summary: Tight first set, epic Estimated/World suite, strong keys
blurb: First set shines with Sugaree, Althea and a crisp Hell In A Bucket opener. The second set delivers an impressive Estimated Prophet > Eyes Of The World > Drums > Space > Morning Dew run, though the overall flow is uneven at times. Brent’s bright keyboards drive the jams while Phil lays down high‑energy bass.
review: The November 22, 1985 show at the Henry J. Kaiser Convention Center captures a band in solid form despite a few rough edges. The first set rolls out with a punchy Hell In A Bucket, followed by standout renditions of Sugaree and Althea that showcase Jerry’s sizzling guitar work even as his voice shows signs of touring wear. Brent Mydland’s keyboards sparkle throughout, especially during the Estimated Prophet jam that rockets into Eyes Of The World, then seamlessly into Drums, Space and a soaring Morning Dew—one of the night’s high points. While the second‑set flow feels uneven to some listeners, moments like the tight Touch of Grey segue and the powerful Turn On Your Lovelight keep the energy high. Recording-wise, the Barbella soundboard (gd85-11-22.sbd.barbella.9709.sbeok.shnf) offers crisp balance with only a slight fader‑mode echo on vocals, making it the recommended source for experiencing this performance.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Three of the four recordings are clearly positive, praising the first‑set tightness, the epic Estimated Prophet > Eyes Of The World suite, and Brent’s keyboard work, while only one is mixed with a minor complaint about a short Cassidy jam. Consistent high praise across multiple sources warrants a strong positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard capture with crisp mix, solid balance and minimal echo; highlights the key performances while preserving overall show energy.

key_highlights: Stellar Sugaree and Althea performances in the first set
Epic Estimated Prophet > Eyes Of The World > Drums > Space > Morning Dew suite with tight segues
Possibly best‑ever Hell In A Bucket rendition noted by fans
Bright, driving keyboard work from Brent Mydland powering the jams

band_performance.Jerry: Guitar is right on and sizzling throughout; vocals show wear from touring but still carry the songs.
band_performance.Phil: In "combat mode," dropping bass bombs with high energy that fuels the band’s drive.
band_performance.Bob: Vocal on Estimated Prophet highlighted as a key element that rockets into the Neverland‑style jam.
band_performance.Brent: Keyboard work praised for bright, clean tone and driving the Estimated jam and overall second‑set flow.
